_comment: Canonical schema for data/postmortems/<id>.json. Open-FEM-Agent §3.2 / Table 1 + task #46 pitfall-DB-first discipline. The retrieval tool in src/tools/consolidated.py (_load_matching_postmortems) and tests/test_postmortem_retrieval.py both enforce this shape; the canonical schema lives here so future authors do not have to read code to know the contract.

id.description: Short kebab-case slug used as the filename stem (without .json). Stable identifier the agent references in breadcrumbs.

date.description: ISO-8601 date the post-mortem was written (YYYY-MM-DD).

backend.description: Exactly one of the seven canonical MCP backends; case-sensitive.

physics.description: Physics-catalog key, e.g. 'linear_elasticity'. Batch records covering multiple physics use a comma-separated list, e.g. 'poisson, heat, helmholtz, eigenvalue'.

surface_symptom.description: What the agent or the user observed first. Diagnostic field — for human review of why the post-mortem was written. NOT a pre-execution planning aid.

root_cause.description: The underlying invariant that was violated. Diagnostic; not retrieval-keyed.

categories.description: Open-FEM-Agent Table 1 categories the failure belongs to. Multiple allowed when the bug spans categories.

pitfall_db_entries.description: The exact strings (with [Category] prefix) added to the relevant KNOWLEDGE['pitfalls'] list as part of this fix. Each entry SHOULD include a 'Signal:' clause stating the observable symptom — that is the string the post-execution critic matches against.

agent_detection_after_fix.description: How the agent now catches this earlier — which knowledge surface, which signal, which critic gate. The 'what changed' from the agent's perspective.

source_refs.description: PR numbers, commit hashes, paper sections, upstream issue links — the audit trail for this record.
